revisionDesc.change: Setting up structure and populating with new tokens

div.head: Unclassified
div.p: This is a fallback value, also understood to apply when @ref is not present on a <g> element.

list.label: identifier
name
description
list.item: a glyph that has not been classified, usually because it is indistinct, or because no visual documentation is available.
list.label: mapping

item.p: Distinguish from short bars and double bars.

list.label: identifier
description
list.item: vertical bar with a headmark or small horizontal line on top
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: vertical bar with a hook on top
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: vertical bar crossed by a predominantly horizontal line
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: vertical bar with short horizontal appendix at middle on the left
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: vertical bar with more complex ornamentation
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar with a headmark or small horizontal line on top
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar with a hook on top of one or both bars
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar crossed by by one or more further strokes that meet the vertical lines more or less at right angles
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar with short horizontal appendix at middle of left bar
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar with short horizontal appendix at middle of right bar
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar enclosing three dots
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: double vertical bar with more complex ornamentation
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: a short, curved vertical stroke at median height
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: a very short vertical line at median height
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: a short vertical bar raised to head height
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: short and quite straight horizontal line
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: horizontal line with the middle bulging downward
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: horizontal line with the middle bulging upward
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: horizontal line with a hook at one end or the other
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: conspicuously long and quite straight horizontal line
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: two short and quite straight horizontal lines
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: a dash with a pair of dots above and below
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: three predominantly horizontal lines one below the other, including cases where the middle line may be a slightly more complex figure
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: two dots, one above the other, like a visarga
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: three dots arranged in a triangle
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: small circle (noticeably smaller than a typical character body), placed at median height; Cambodian “Chicken egg”
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: large circle (about the size of a typical character body or larger)
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: two circles, one above the other, like a visarga
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: circle topped by a double (or multiple) curved stroke
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: circle containing one or more concentric circles, Cambodian “Chicken eye”
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: circle with a concentric dot, Cambodian “Chicken eye”
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: oval containing one or more concentric circles or ovals
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: three small circles arranged in a triangle
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: a circular shape depicting a wheel with spokes
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: circle with one or more curved lines
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: counter-clockwise (left-turning) spiral (like a mirrored figure 6 or 9)
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: cross in the shape of a + sign
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: cross in the shape of an ×
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: flower-shaped design not matching any particular species of floret
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: floret with four petals, potentially with spikes or smaller petals interspersed between the four cardinal petals
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: floral design with more detail than a centre and petals (including flowers with eight identical petals)
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: final gomūtra rendered as a tapering series of vertical bars
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: final gomūtra involving a more complex design than the archetypal rendering
list.label: mapping
identifier
identifier
description
list.item: a wavy or zigzag vertical line
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: symbol that is definitely present, but is so damaged (or unclear in the surrogate for a reason other than damage) that it cannot be assigned to any of the primary categories
list.label: mapping
identifier
description
list.item: symbol that cannot be assigned to a genus
list.label: mapping

div.head: Sample section
div.p: All divisions must start with a heading.
It is possible to add paragraphs. You can point to a glyph like this.
If you need to refer to an inscription, use the full path of the text on the server, like this. You can also use the full URL, like this.
Below is a record (a list of key/value pairs). The elements "identifier" and "name" can be repeated several times. The other elements should occur only once.

item.p: This will be displayed at the bottom of the card, below the set of fields. Note that block-level elements like "p" must be used, this field should not contain plain text.
Images can be embedded as follows. Their height is capped.
Images can also be inlined but this only makes sense for SVG files.
If you want to link to an image (per contrast with embedding it in the display), use a normal ref element.

div.p: Divisions can nest, as can be seen below.
